title: Quiz: Multi-Task Quantum Learning
description: Test your understanding of multi-task learning in quantum ML

questions.question: What is multi-task learning?

options.text: Training a single model on multiple tasks simultaneously

questions.explanation: Multi-task learning trains a single model on multiple related tasks simultaneously, enabling knowledge sharing across tasks.

questions.question: What is 'hard parameter sharing' in multi-task learning?

options.text: All tasks share the same encoder, with task-specific heads
Each task has completely separate parameters

questions.explanation: Hard parameter sharing means all tasks share a common encoder network, while each task has its own task-specific output head.

questions.question: Why is loss balancing important in multi-task learning?

options.text: To prevent tasks with larger losses from dominating training

questions.explanation: Loss balancing prevents tasks with larger loss magnitudes from dominating the gradient updates, ensuring all tasks receive adequate training.

questions.question: Multi-task learning always improves performance on every task.
questions.explanation: False. Negative transfer can occur when tasks are unrelated or conflict, potentially hurting some tasks' performance.

questions.question: What are auxiliary tasks used for?

options.text: To improve main task performance through related supervision
To reduce training data needs to zero

questions.explanation: Auxiliary tasks provide additional related supervision that helps learn better representations for the main task.

questions.question: What is uncertainty weighting in loss balancing?

options.text: Learning task weights based on prediction uncertainty

questions.explanation: Uncertainty weighting learns a weight for each task based on the model's prediction uncertainty, automatically balancing task contributions.

questions.question: What is transfer gain in multi-task evaluation?

options.text: Performance improvement from multi-task vs single-task training

questions.explanation: Transfer gain measures the performance improvement achieved by multi-task learning compared to training each task in isolation.

questions.question: Which are valid parameter sharing strategies? (Select all)
questions.explanation: Valid strategies include hard sharing, soft sharing with regularization, and cross-stitch networks. Completely independent training is not multi-task learning.
